panels.title: Disk Usage %
panels.description: cloudberry_disk_usage_percent{cluster,namespace} (Scenario 116 / S.1+M.1+R.2). Measured each reconcile by reconcileStorage->recordDiskUsage when diskMonitoring=true: prefers true filesystem usage via gp_toolkit.gp_disk_free, falling back to a logical-size-vs-provisioned-capacity proxy (sum(pg_database_size) / segment PVC capacity) when gp_disk_free is unavailable. The gauge is published from the same value written to status.diskUsagePercent (M.1 == S.1) and tracks growth as data is loaded.

panels.title: Backup Last Status
panels.description: Last backup status per cluster (0=Success, 1=Failed, 2=In-Progress)

panels.title: Time Since Last Successful Backup
panels.description: Elapsed time since the last successful backup per cluster

panels.title: Restore Duration p99/p50
panels.description: Restore operation duration percentiles

panels.title: Backup Retention Deleted
panels.description: Number of backups deleted by retention policy over time

panels.title: Backup Job Status
panels.description: Kubernetes backup Job status per job and operation (0=pending, 1=running, 2=succeeded, 3=failed)

panels.title: Backup Duration by Type (p99)
panels.description: Backup duration p99 split by backup type (full vs incremental) from cloudberry_backup_duration_seconds{type}
Number of external PXF servers declared in the spec (len(pxf.servers)), set by reconcileDataLoading when pxf.enabled. Config-derived, NOT a liveness/health signal.
panels.title: PXF Servers Configured

panels.description: Observed PXF runtime status derived from segment-primary pxf container readiness: 0=Stopped, 1=Running, 2=Error. ABSENT when unobservable (no segment pods or no pxf containers). From cloudberry_pxf_status{cluster,namespace}.
panels.title: PXF Status

panels.description: Number of PXF client extensions (pxf, pxf_fdw) observed in pg_extension via live probe. ABSENT when the DB is unreachable or no PXF extensions are installed. From cloudberry_pxf_extensions_installed{cluster,namespace}.
panels.title: PXF Extensions Installed
Retention Deletions (1h)
panels.description: Backups deleted by retention policy in the last 1h per cluster (Scenario 79 retention cleanup activity) from cloudberry_backup_retention_deleted_total

panels.title: Restore Operations by Result
panels.description: Restore operations rate broken down by result (success, error, partial)

panels.title: Partial Restores (1h)
panels.description: Number of partial restore operations in the last hour

panels.title: Failed Restores (1h)
panels.description: Number of failed restore operations in the last hour

Rate of admission webhook DENIED decisions from cloudberry_webhook_admission_total{webhook,operation,result="denied"}. The validating webhook enforces the data-loading rules W.1–W.25 (Scenario 110: pxf.image required, server name/type/config, job name/type, pxfJob server/profile/targetTable, gpload targetTable, cron schedule, partitioning, segmentRejectLimitType) plus the storage-recommendation rules W.1–W.4 (Scenario 113: storage.recommendationScan.bloatThreshold/skewThreshold/indexBloatThreshold must be 0–100 and ageThreshold must be non-negative — enabled-gated, webhook-authoritative) plus cluster-uniqueness/mirroring. Each rejected CR is denied with a descriptive error and does NOT persist. W.3/W.8/W.15 (data-loading) are additionally guarded by CRD OpenAPI enums.
panels.title: Webhook Denied Rate
Data Loading Job Status
panels.description: Per-job data loading status from cloudberry_data_loading_job_status (0=idle/pending,1=running,2=success,3=failed); derived from the spawned Job's Kubernetes status.

panels.title: Data Loading Rows Loaded
panels.description: Total rows loaded by data loading jobs by source_type, from cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total (harvested from the Job pod's DATALOAD_ROWS termination marker).

panels.title: Data Loading Job Duration p95
panels.description: p95 wall-clock duration of data loading Job runs per job, from the cloudberry_data_loading_job_duration_seconds histogram.

panels.title: Data Loading Errors
panels.description: Rate of failed data loading Job runs per job, from cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total (incremented on terminal Failed Job state).

panels.title: Data Loading Last Success
panels.description: Time since the last successful data loading Job run per job, derived from cloudberry_data_loading_job_last_success_timestamp (Unix seconds).

panels.title: PXF Restarts
panels.description: Rate of PXF restart operations triggered via cloudberry-ctl pxf restart / POST data-loading/pxf/restart, from cloudberry_pxf_restart_total{cluster,namespace,result}. result=started on a successful StatefulSet restart-trigger bump (rolls the segment pods and their PXF sidecars), result=failed otherwise. Counts operator-issued restarts, NOT in-place sidecar restarts.

panels.title: PXF Server Config Changes
panels.description: Rate of PXF server-configuration changes from cloudberry_pxf_servers_changed_total{cluster,namespace}. The operator increments this counter ONLY when the <cluster>-pxf-servers ConfigMap Data actually changes (a server in dataLoading.pxf.servers[] was added, updated, or removed) — Scenario 106 (SL.7 update / SL.8 delete). Never incremented on a no-op reconcile. A corresponding Kubernetes Event 'PXFServersChanged' (added=[..] removed=[..] updated=[..]) is emitted on the controller reconcile path.

panels.title: Data Loading Rows by Source Type
panels.description: Rows-loaded rate aggregated purely by the real source_type label of cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total (s3/hdfs/hive/jdbc/gpfdist/file). Highlights object-store (s3) loads (Scenario 96). source_type is derived from the data-loading Job's server/source type; the value is harvested from the Job pod's DATALOAD_ROWS termination marker (never synthesized).

panels.title: Object Store Profiles (Scenario 96)
panels.description: Documents the PXF object-store format write-capability matrix. The read-vs-write distinction is enforced at admission/build time, NOT measured at runtime, so per-profile read/write counts have no operator metric and are marked Planned (metrics-honesty).

options.content:
## Object Store Profiles — Format Write-Capability (Scenario 96)

The object-store profile a table uses determines whether it can be **written** (INSERT/external write) or is **read-only**. This is enforced at **admission / build time** (webhook + profile resolution), **not** measured by the runtime — so there is **no operator metric** for per-profile read/write counts.

| Format  | Read | Write | Notes |
|---------|:----:|:-----:|-------|
| text    |  ✅  |  ✅   | writable |
| parquet |  ✅  |  ✅   | writable |
| avro    |  ✅  |  ✅   | writable |
| json    |  ✅  |  ❌   | read-only |
| orc     |  ✅  |  ❌   | read-only |

**Honest metric coverage on this dashboard**

- **Real**: object-store loads are visible via the real `source_type` label on `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total` — see the *Data Loading Rows by Source Type* panel (`s3` = object store). Job status / duration / errors / last-success use the real `cloudberry_data_loading_*` families.
- **Real (config-derived)**: `cloudberry_pxf_servers_configured` = `len(pxf.servers)` (declared, not live-reachability). `cloudberry_pxf_restart_total{cluster,namespace,result}` counts operator-issued PXF restarts.
- **Planned (no runtime metric yet)**: per-profile **read/write** counts and the write-capability decision are enforced at admission/build time and are **not emitted** by the runtime. No fake panel is shown for them.

panels.title: Hadoop Data Loading Rows (HDFS/Hive/HBase)
panels.description: Rows-loaded rate filtered to Hadoop source types (hdfs, hive, hbase) from cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total{source_type=~"hdfs|hive|hbase"}. Scenario 97 Hadoop Profiles: source_type is derived from the data-loading Job's PXF server type; the value is harvested from the Job pod's DATALOAD_ROWS termination marker (never synthesized).

panels.title: Hadoop Profiles (Scenario 97)
panels.description: Documents the HDFS/Hive/HBase read profiles and write-capability matrix. The read-vs-write distinction is enforced at admission/build time (scheme-aware policy fix), NOT measured at runtime, so per-profile read/write COUNTS remain Planned (no runtime metric).

options.content:
## Hadoop Profiles (Scenario 97)

### HDFS Profiles
| Profile | Read | Write | Notes |
|---------|:----:|:-----:|-------|
| hdfs:text | Yes | Yes | writable |
| hdfs:parquet | Yes | Yes | writable |
| hdfs:avro | Yes | Yes | writable |
| hdfs:sequencefile | Yes | Yes | writable (FF.7) |
| hdfs:json | Yes | No | read-only (scheme-aware fix) |
| hdfs:orc | Yes | No | read-only (scheme-aware fix) |

### Hive Profiles
| Profile | Read | Write | Notes |
|---------|:----:|:-----:|-------|
| hive (auto-detect) | Yes | No | read-only |
| hive:text | Yes | No | read-only |
| hive:orc | Yes | No | read-only |
| hive:rc | Yes | No | read-only |

### HBase
| Profile | Read | Write | Notes |
|---------|:----:|:-----:|-------|
| HBase | Yes | No | read-only |

### Metric Coverage
- **Real**: Hadoop loads visible via `source_type` label on `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total` (hdfs/hive/hbase). See *Hadoop Data Loading Rows* panel.
- **Real**: `cloudberry_pxf_servers_configured` counts declared PXF servers (config-derived).
- **Planned**: Per-profile read/write COUNTS are NOT emitted by the runtime (enforced at admission/build time).

panels.title: Writable External Tables / Data Export (Scenario 99)
panels.description: Documents writable export to S3/HDFS/JDBC via CREATE WRITABLE EXTERNAL TABLE ... FORMATTER='pxfwritable_export'. Export success is observed via existing data-loading metrics. No new operator metric was added; cloudberry_pxf_bytes_transferred_total remains PLANNED.

options.content:
## Writable External Tables / Data Export (Scenario 99)

### Overview

Scenario 99 adds **writable export** to S3, HDFS, and JDBC via `CREATE WRITABLE EXTERNAL TABLE ... FORMATTER='pxfwritable_export'`. The operator generates a reversed INSERT (`INSERT INTO <writable_ext> SELECT * FROM <sourceTable>`) and the new `PxfJobSpec.SourceFilter` field enables filtered exports (`WHERE region='us-east'`).

### Export Jobs (sample CR: `scenario99-export-test.yaml`)

| Job | Profile | Target | Notes |
|-----|---------|--------|-------|
| **FE.9** s3-export | s3:text writable | `cloudberry-warehouse/exports/s3/` (MinIO) | Full export |
| **FE.10** hdfs-export | hdfs:text writable | `/data-lake/exports/hdfs/` (HDFS) | Full export |
| **FE.11** jdbc-export | jdbc writable | `export_target` (pgsource) | Strongest proof: rows land in RDBMS |
| **SF.1** s3-export-filtered | s3:text writable + `sourceFilter` | `cloudberry-warehouse/exports/s3-filtered/` | `WHERE region='us-east'` lands fewer rows |

### Observability via Real Metrics

Export jobs are data-loading jobs with `mode: writable`. They use the **same existing metrics** as read jobs:

| Signal | Metric | How It Proves Export |
|--------|--------|---------------------|
| Rows exported | `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total{source_type}` | Counter incremented from DATALOAD_ROWS termination marker |
| Export success | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status` = **2** | Job reached terminal Succeeded state |
| Export failure | `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total` | Incremented on terminal Failed state |
| Export duration | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_duration_seconds` | Wall-clock from Job start/completion |
| Last success | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_last_success_timestamp` | Unix timestamp of last successful run |
| Active jobs | `cloudberry_data_loading_jobs_active` | Gauge of running data-loading jobs |

### Filtered Export (SourceFilter / SF.1)

The `sourceFilter` field on `PxfJobSpec` appends a `WHERE` clause to the reversed INSERT. The filtered export (`s3-export-filtered`) lands **fewer rows** than the unfiltered baseline (`s3-export`), observable as a lower `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total` count.

### Landing Proofs (e2e Part B)

| Target | Proof Method |
|--------|--------------|
| S3 (MinIO) | Object listing: `exports/s3/` contains part files |
| HDFS | WebHDFS LISTSTATUS: `/data-lake/exports/hdfs/` has part files |
| JDBC (pgsource) | `SELECT count(*) FROM export_target > 0` |

### What Is NOT Measured (Planned)

- **`cloudberry_pxf_bytes_transferred_total`** remains **PLANNED**. PXF has no honest external-bytes counter; the metric is not registered in the operator. No panel on this dashboard references it.
- Per-profile write counts are enforced at admission/build time, not measured at runtime.

### Dashboard Panels (already present in this row)

The existing Data Loading panels cover export jobs because export jobs ARE data-loading jobs:
- **Data Loading Job Status** (id 251): shows export job status (idle/running/success/failed)
- **Data Loading Rows Loaded** (id 252): shows rows exported by source_type
- **Data Loading Errors** (id 254): shows export job failures
- **Data Loading Job Duration p95** (id 253): shows export job duration
- **Data Loading Last Success** (id 255): shows time since last successful export

panels.title: kafka-cdc Continuous Streaming (Scenario 102)
panels.description: Documents the kafka custom-connector profile, pxf-connector-init JAR download, continuous one-off Job, and how it is observed via existing cloudberry_data_loading_* metrics. No new operator metric was added for Scenario 102.

options.content:
## kafka-cdc Continuous Streaming (Scenario 102)

### Architecture

| Component | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| **Custom Connector Profile** | `servers[].type: custom` + `customConnectors[].jarUrl` in the CR (W.3 + W.24) |
| **pxf-connector-init** | Init container (C.18) downloads the JAR from `s3://cloudberry-data/connectors/kafka-connector.jar` into `/pxf/lib/custom/kafka-connector.jar` in the PXF sidecar |
| **Continuous Job** | A one-off long-running Job (**NOT** a CronJob, J.46) — `continuous: true`, no `schedule` field |
| **Consume Loop** | `batchSize: 10000` / `flushInterval: 30s` drive the streaming consume via `CBK_BATCH_SIZE` / `CBK_FLUSH_INTERVAL` env vars |
| **External Table** | `pxf://cloudberry-cdc?PROFILE=kafka&SERVER=kafka-connector` -> `public.kafka_events` |
| **Sample CR** | `scenario102-kafka-test.yaml` (cluster `kafka-test`, namespace `cloudberry-test`) |

### Observability — HONEST Metric Coverage

Scenario 102 added **NO new operator metric**. The kafka-cdc job reuses the existing `cloudberry_data_loading_*` family:

| Signal | Metric | Streaming Interpretation |
|--------|--------|--------------------------|
| Job status | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status` | **1 = Running is the EXPECTED steady state** for a continuous streaming consumer. Unlike batch jobs that reach 2 (success/Complete), a streaming consumer stays Running indefinitely. A value of 3 (failed) indicates the consumer crashed. |
| Rows loaded | `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total{source_type}` | Best-effort per flush — incremented from the Job pod's `DATALOAD_ROWS` termination marker (harvested on flush boundaries, not on every message) |
| Active jobs | `cloudberry_data_loading_jobs_active` | Gauge includes the kafka-cdc job while Running |
| Errors | `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total` | Incremented if the Job reaches terminal Failed state |
| Duration | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_duration_seconds` | Not meaningful for a continuous job (no completion time) |
| Last success | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_last_success_timestamp` | Not set for a continuous job (never completes) |

> **Key insight**: A Running (not Complete) status is the **EXPECTED steady state** for a streaming consumer. The existing *Data Loading Job Status* panel (id 251, state-timeline) already shows the kafka-cdc job's persistent Running (blue) state — no duplicate panel needed.

### Planned Metrics (NOT registered)

- `cloudberry_pxf_bytes_transferred_total` — **PLANNED** (requires live PXF probing)
- `cloudberry_gpfdist_*` — **PLANNED** (gpfdist-specific, not applicable to kafka-cdc)

No panel references `pxf_bytes_transferred_total` or `gpfdist_*` expressions — they are not registered in the operator.

panels.title: Filter Pushdown & Projection (Scenario 98)
panels.description: Documents how filter pushdown, column projection, and per-row error handling (Scenario 98) are observed via real operator metrics. No bytes_transferred metric exists (Planned).

options.content:
## Filter Pushdown, Column Projection & Per-Row Error Handling (Scenario 98)

### Observability via Real Metrics

| Feature | How Observed | Metric / Method |
|---------|-------------|----------------|
| **FE.1 Filter Pushdown (Parquet)** | Row-count reduction: `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total` for the filtered job lands **fewer rows** than the unfiltered baseline job | `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total{source_type="s3"}` |
| **FE.2 Filter Pushdown (JDBC)** | Row-count reduction + source query logs show pushed WHERE predicate | `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total{source_type="jdbc"}` + EXPLAIN |
| **FE.3 Filter Pushdown (Hive)** | CONFIG-ONLY (no live Hive backing); EXPLAIN shows predicate pushdown | EXPLAIN output (no runtime metric) |
| **FE.4 Column Projection (Parquet)** | EXPLAIN shows only projected columns read from external table | EXPLAIN output (no runtime metric for bytes saved) |
| **FE.5 Column Projection (ORC)** | CONFIG-ONLY; same EXPLAIN-based proof | EXPLAIN output |
| **FE.12a Error Handling (tolerate)** | Job completes despite bad rows: `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status` = **2** (success) | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status{job="fe12a-malformed-tolerated"}` |
| **FE.12b Error Handling (fail)** | Job fails when bad rows exceed limit: `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status` = **3** (failed), `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total` increments | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status{job="fe12b-malformed-failed"}` + `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total` |

### Dashboard Panels (already present in this row)

- **Data Loading Job Status** (id 251): state-timeline showing per-job status (idle/running/success/failed) — directly shows FE.12a success vs FE.12b failure.
- **Data Loading Errors** (id 254): `rate(cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total[5m])` — shows FE.12b error-rate spike.
- **Data Loading Rows Loaded** (id 252): total rows by source_type — filtered job lands fewer rows than baseline (FE.1/FE.2 proof).
- **Data Loading Job Duration p95** (id 253): duration comparison between filtered and unfiltered jobs.

### What is NOT Measured (Planned)

- **`cloudberry_pxf_bytes_transferred_total`**: **PLANNED** — PXF 2.1.0 has no honest external-bytes counter. No panel references this metric. Per-profile bytes transferred cannot be measured until PXF exposes a reliable counter.
- **Per-profile read/write counts**: enforced at admission/build time, not emitted by the runtime.
- **Column projection bytes saved**: observable only via EXPLAIN, not via a runtime metric.

### Proof Strategy

Filter pushdown and column projection are proven by:
1. **Row-count reduction** (`cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total` filtered < unfiltered baseline)
2. **EXPLAIN** output showing predicate/projection pushdown
3. **Source-side query logs** (e.g., PostgreSQL `log_statement='all'` shows the pushed WHERE)
4. **Job status** (`cloudberry_data_loading_job_status` 2=success / 3=failed for error handling)

panels.title: FDW-Based Loading Path (Scenario 103)
panels.description: Documents the pxf_fdw FDW loading path (loadMethod:fdw), how it maps to per-protocol FDW wrappers, and how it is observed via existing cloudberry_data_loading_* metrics. No new operator metric was added for Scenario 103.

options.content:
## FDW-Based Loading Path (Scenario 103)

### Architecture

Scenario 103 adds an **alternative FDW loading path** alongside the existing external-table path. The CR field `pxfJob.loadMethod` selects the mechanism:

| `loadMethod` | DDL Generated | Mechanism |
|-------------|--------------|----------|
| `external-table` (default) | `CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE ... LOCATION('pxf://...')` | Traditional PXF external table |
| `fdw` | `CREATE SERVER` + `CREATE USER MAPPING` + `CREATE FOREIGN TABLE` | pxf_fdw Foreign Data Wrapper |

### Per-Protocol FDW Wrappers (EX.5-EX.8)

When `loadMethod: fdw`, the operator generates DDL using the **per-protocol pxf_fdw wrappers** registered in the DB image:

| Protocol | FDW Wrapper | Example |
|----------|------------|--------|
| S3 | `s3_pxf_fdw` | `CREATE SERVER s3srv FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER s3_pxf_fdw OPTIONS (...)` |
| JDBC | `jdbc_pxf_fdw` | `CREATE SERVER jdbcsrv FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER jdbc_pxf_fdw OPTIONS (...)` |
| HDFS | `hdfs_pxf_fdw` | `CREATE SERVER hdfssrv FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER hdfs_pxf_fdw OPTIONS (...)` |
| Hive | `hive_pxf_fdw` | `CREATE SERVER hivesrv FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER hive_pxf_fdw OPTIONS (...)` |
| HBase | `hbase_pxf_fdw` | `CREATE SERVER hbasesrv FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER hbase_pxf_fdw OPTIONS (...)` |
| Custom | `pxf_fdw_handler` (generic) | Fallback for custom connector profiles |

The FDW path generates: `CREATE SERVER` (with PXF endpoint + profile options) -> `CREATE USER MAPPING` -> `CREATE FOREIGN TABLE` (column-mapped) -> `INSERT INTO <target> SELECT * FROM <foreign_table>` [+ optional `WHERE` from `sourceFilter`].

### Sample CR: `scenario103-fdw-test.yaml`

| Job | loadMethod | Target Table | Purpose |
|-----|-----------|-------------|--------|
| `s3-ext-load` | external-table (default) | `public.events_ext` | Baseline: external-table path |
| `s3-fdw-load` | **fdw** | `public.events_fdw` | FDW path over SAME s3 dataset |
| `s3-fdw-filtered` | **fdw** + `sourceFilter` | `public.events_fdw_filtered` | Filtered FDW load (fewer rows) |

### Equivalence Proof (EX.8)

The headline proof is **row-count equivalence**: `count(events_ext) == count(events_fdw)` over the SAME s3 dataset (`cloudberry-data/text/data.csv`, 1000 rows). The filtered FDW load (`s3-fdw-filtered`) lands FEWER rows due to `sourceFilter: "value > 500"`. This is proven by **ROW COUNTS**, not a new metric.

### Observability -- HONEST Metric Coverage

Scenario 103 added **NO new operator metric**. An FDW load IS a data-loading job -- it reuses the existing `cloudberry_data_loading_*` family:

| Signal | Metric | FDW Interpretation |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| Job status | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status` | 0=idle, 1=running, 2=success, 3=failed (same as external-table) |
| Rows loaded | `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total{source_type}` | Counter from DATALOAD_ROWS termination marker (FDW INSERT row count) |
| Errors | `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total` | Incremented on terminal Failed Job state |
| Duration | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_duration_seconds` | Wall-clock from Job start/completion |
| Last success | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_last_success_timestamp` | Unix timestamp of last successful run |
| Active jobs | `cloudberry_data_loading_jobs_active` | Gauge includes FDW jobs while Running |

The existing **Data Loading** panels in this row (Job Status id 251, Rows Loaded id 252, Duration id 253, Errors id 254, Last Success id 255) already display FDW jobs -- no duplicate panel needed.

### Planned Metrics (NOT registered)

- `cloudberry_pxf_bytes_transferred_total` -- would measure bytes read/written per profile; requires PXF JMX integration. **Not registered**.
- `cloudberry_gpfdist_*` -- gpfdist-specific metrics. **Not registered**.
- Per-protocol FDW wrapper counts -- not emitted by the runtime.

panels.title: Pre-Load Health Checks (Scenario 104)
panels.description: Documents the dataload-healthcheck init container (HC.1-HC.5), how failures are observed via real metrics and kube-state-metrics, and the DataLoadingHealthCheckFailed Kubernetes Event.

options.content:
## Pre-Load Health Checks (Scenario 104)

### Architecture

The `dataload-healthcheck` init container runs on **BOTH** PXF and gpload data-loading Job pods when `dataLoading.healthChecks.enabled: true`. It performs 5 sequential checks before the main load container starts:

| Check | Name | What It Probes | Failure Mode |
|-------|------|---------------|-------------|
| **HC.1** | PXF DB-proxy readiness | Coordinator can reach PXF on segment primaries (DB-proxy probe, not direct sidecar curl) | Init exits non-zero |
| **HC.2** | Target table exists | `SELECT 1 FROM <targetTable> LIMIT 0` via coordinator | Init exits non-zero |
| **HC.3** | Source connectivity | Curl-HEAD the PXF server's endpoint (e.g. `fs.s3a.endpoint` for S3) | Init exits non-zero |
| **HC.4** | gpfdist reachability | Curl the gpfdist Service (`<cluster>-gpfdist-svc:<port>/`) | Init exits non-zero (gpload jobs only) |
| **HC.5** | Disk space | Check free MB on `/dataload-scratch` >= `diskMinFreeMB` | Init exits non-zero |

### Failure Signals (ALL REAL)

When any HC fails, the init container exits non-zero, which causes the **Job to fail**. This produces:

| Signal | Source | Metric / Event |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| Job status = Failed | Operator metric | `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status` = **3** |
| Error counter | Operator metric | `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total` increments |
| Kubernetes Event | Operator (EventRecorder) | **DataLoadingHealthCheckFailed** Warning Event (de-duplicated) |
| Job failure count | kube-state-metrics | `kube_job_status_failed{job_name=~".*-dataload-.*"}` |
| Init container status | kube-state-metrics | `kube_pod_init_container_status_terminated_reason{reason="Error"}` |
| gpfdist Deployment readiness (HC.4) | kube-state-metrics | `kube_deployment_status_replicas_available{deployment=~".*-gpfdist"}` |

### CRD Knob

```yaml
dataLoading:
  healthChecks:
    enabled: true          # default: true
    diskMinFreeMB: 64      # HC.5 threshold
    scratchSizeLimit: 64Mi # emptyDir sizeLimit for /dataload-scratch
```

### Metric Honesty

Scenario 104 added **NO new operator metric**. HC failures are observed via the **existing** `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status=3` + `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total` + the `DataLoadingHealthCheckFailed` Event + the **NEW kube-state-metrics** (`kube_job_status_failed`, `kube_pod_init_container_status_*`, `kube_deployment_status_replicas_available`). The `cloudberry_pxf_*` / `cloudberry_gpfdist_*` families remain **PLANNED** and are never asserted.

panels.title: Data Loading Job Failures (kube-state-metrics)
panels.description: Failed data-loading Kubernetes Jobs from kube-state-metrics (kube_job_status_failed). Filters to jobs matching *-dataload-* naming convention. A spike indicates a health-check init failure (Scenario 104) or a load execution failure. This is a genuinely NEW panel enabled by kube-state-metrics deployment.

panels.title: gpfdist Deployment Ready Replicas (kube-state-metrics)
panels.description: Available replicas of gpfdist Deployments from kube-state-metrics (kube_deployment_status_replicas_available). HC.4 probes the gpfdist Service; when replicas drop to 0 the health check fails. This panel was previously impossible without kube-state-metrics.

panels.title: Recommendations by Type
panels.description: cloudberry_recommendations_total{type} (Scenario 117 / M.2). recordRecommendations runs all four threshold-aware scans each reconcile when recommendationScan is enabled and publishes a gauge per type: bloat (RT.1/C.6, dead_pct>=bloatThreshold), skew (RT.2/C.7, skew coeff>=skewThreshold), age (RT.3/C.8, age(relfrozenxid)>=ageThreshold), index_bloat (RT.4/C.9, bloat_pct>=indexBloatThreshold). All four types are published (including 0 when a type clears), and the sum equals status.recommendationCount (S.2/R.4 — the M.2==count invariant). table_bloat_ratio reflects the bloat ratio for the top tables (M.4).

panels.title: Recommendation Scan Duration
panels.description: cloudberry_recommendation_scan_duration_seconds histogram (Scenario 118 / M.3): the operator observes the elapsed time of each recommendation scan in recordRecommendations. The scan is capped at spec.storage.recommendationScan.scanDuration (C.10, resolveScanDuration: default 10s, clamp 24h, else verbatim) applied to the four Get* queries (the DB connection has a separate fixed budget). When a scan hits the cap it records partial results and increments cloudberry_recommendation_scan_truncated_total (the 'truncated/s' series here) and sets status.recommendationScanTruncated; the duration metric reflects the capped run.

panels.title: Recommendation Scan CronJob (Scenario 115 / C.5)
panels.description: cloudberry_recommendation_scan_cronjob gauge per cluster: 1 when the operator's reconcileStorage (R.1 diskMonitoring gate) has ensured the <cluster>-recommendation-scan CronJob (C.5) for the recommendationScan.schedule, 0 when GC'd (disabled). Pairs with the StorageConfigured=True condition (R.5).

panels.title: Monitoring Disabled Access (24h)
panels.description: Number of times monitoring endpoints were accessed while monitoring was disabled

panels.title: Certificate Rotation Rate
panels.description: Rate of certificate rotation events by component, source, and result

panels.title: Certificate Expiry
panels.description: Seconds until certificate expires per component. Red below 7 days, yellow below 30 days.

panels.title: Vault Operations Rate
panels.description: Rate of Vault operations (auth, read, write) by result

panels.title: Vault Operation Duration
panels.description: Vault operation latency percentiles (p50, p99)

panels.title: Webhook Admissions Rate
panels.description: Rate of admission webhook decisions by webhook type, operation, and result from cloudberry_webhook_admission_total. The validating webhook enforces W-rules (data-loading W.1-W.25 / Scenario 110 + storage-recommendation W.1-W.4 / Scenario 113), recording result=denied on rejection. The mutating webhook injects defaults (data-loading D.1-D.14 / Scenario 90 + storage-recommendation D.1-D.6 / Scenario 114 for recommendationScan schedule/bloatThreshold/skewThreshold/ageThreshold/indexBloatThreshold/scanDuration), recording webhook=mutating,result=allowed on every defaulting pass (defaulting never denies).

panels.title: Webhook Denied (1h)
panels.description: Webhook admission requests denied in the last hour

panels.title: Webhook Errors (1h)
panels.description: Webhook admission errors in the last hour

panels.title: Upgrade Operations
panels.description: Rate of cluster upgrade operations by result (started, completed, rollback, failed)

panels.title: Rolling Restarts
panels.description: Rate of rolling restart operations by result (started, completed, failed)

panels.title: Recovery Operations
panels.description: Rate of recovery operations by type (incremental, full, differential) and result

panels.title: Cluster TLS Cert Issuance Rate
panels.description: Rate of CloudberryCluster server certificate issuances and renewals from Vault PKI

panels.title: Cert Issuances (1h)
panels.description: Total cluster TLS certificate issuances in the last hour by result

panels.title: Cert Issuance Errors (1h)
panels.description: Failed cluster TLS certificate issuances in the last hour

panels.title: Upgrades Failed (1h)
panels.description: Failed upgrade operations in the last hour

panels.title: Upgrade Rollbacks (1h)
panels.description: Upgrade rollback operations in the last hour

panels.title: Rolling Restarts Failed (1h)
panels.description: Failed rolling restart operations in the last hour

panels.title: Recovery Failed (1h)
panels.description: Failed recovery operations in the last hour

panels.title: Cert Rotation Errors (1h)
panels.description: Certificate rotation errors in the last hour

panels.title: Vault Errors (1h)
panels.description: Vault operation errors in the last hour

panels.title: Backup & Restore Health (Scenario 77 Pre-Backup Checks / Scenario 80 Post-Restore Validation)
panels.description: Surfaces backup-Job health so pre-backup-check failures (segments-down, long-running txn, S3 unreachable, low disk) are immediately visible. Backup blocked => Job Failed (status=3), backup_last_status=Failed (1) and backup_total{result="failed"} increments. Also surfaces Scenario 80 post-restore validation outcomes (cloudberry_restore_validation_total{result=success|failed}): the validation Job compares restored row counts vs gpbackup history, runs an invalid-index scan and a health-check query, and flags any discrepancy.

panels.title: Failed Backup Jobs (current)
panels.description: Number of backup Jobs currently in the Failed state (cloudberry_backup_job_status == 3, operation=backup). A non-zero value indicates a Scenario 77 pre-backup-check block or a failed backup execution.

panels.title: Backups Failed (1h)
panels.description: Backup operations that failed in the last hour (cloudberry_backup_total{result="failed"}). Each Scenario 77 pre-backup-check block increments this counter.

panels.title: Last Backup Status
panels.description: Most recent backup status per cluster (cloudberry_backup_last_status: 0=Success, 1=Failed, 2=In-Progress). Failed means a pre-backup check blocked the backup or the backup itself failed.

panels.title: Last Successful Backup
panels.description: Wall-clock time of the last successful backup per cluster (cloudberry_backup_last_success_timestamp, Unix seconds).

panels.title: Time Since Last Successful Backup
panels.description: Elapsed time since the last successful backup per cluster (time() - cloudberry_backup_last_success_timestamp). Grows while pre-backup checks keep blocking backups.

panels.title: Backup Job Status Over Time
panels.description: Per-Job backup status timeline (cloudberry_backup_job_status, operation=backup). Codes: 0=Pending, 1=Running, 2=Succeeded, 3=Failed. A drop to 3 marks a Scenario 77 pre-backup-check block or a failed backup run.

panels.title: Backup Outcomes Rate (success vs failed)
panels.description: Rate of backup outcomes by result (cloudberry_backup_total). Rising failed series indicates recurring Scenario 77 pre-backup-check blocks.

panels.title: Restore Validations Failed (1h)
panels.description: Post-restore validation failures in the last hour (cloudberry_restore_validation_total{result="failed"}). Scenario 80: a non-zero value means the validation Job flagged an issue (e.g. row-count mismatch vs gpbackup history, invalid index, or failed health check).

panels.title: Restore Validation (1h)
panels.description: Post-restore validation outcomes by result in the last hour (cloudberry_restore_validation_total{result=success|failed}). Scenario 80 records this from the validation Job's terminal status.

panels.title: Restore Validation Outcomes (success vs failed)
panels.description: Post-restore validation outcomes over time by result (cloudberry_restore_validation_total{result}). Scenario 80: the failed series rises when validation flags a row-count mismatch vs gpbackup history, an invalid index, or a failed health-check query.

panels.title: Restore/Migration Outcomes Rate (success vs failed)
panels.description: Rate of restore outcomes by result (cloudberry_restore_total{result}). Scenario 87 cross-cluster migration runs gprestore on the target as a restore; a rising failed series indicates the migration restore step is failing.

panels.title: Restore Duration p95 (Migration)
panels.description: 95th percentile restore (gprestore) duration from cloudberry_restore_duration_seconds_bucket. Tracks how long the Scenario 87 migration restore step takes on the target cluster.

panels.title: Data-Loading API Requests (P.1–P.15)
panels.description: Request rate for the data-loading REST API surface from cloudberry_api_requests_total{route,method,code}, route=~'.*/data-loading/.*'. Covers Scenario 107 endpoints (P.1–P.15) AND Scenario 108 CLI commands (L.1–L.16) — the cloudberry-ctl pxf servers/jobs/test-read subcommands all flow through these same routes, so this reflects both REST and CLI-driven traffic + status codes (2xx, 409 conflict/PXF_NOT_READY, 400 VALIDATION_FAILED, 404).

panels.title: Storage API Requests (P.1–P.6)
panels.description: Request rate + status for the storage REST API surface from cloudberry_api_requests_total{route,method,code}, route=~'.*/storage/.*'. Covers Scenario 119 endpoints: P.1 GET disk-usage (diskUsagePercent==status + per-segment breakdown), P.2 GET tables (size/bloat/skew via GetTables), P.3 GET tables/{schema}/{table} (detail + indexSizes), P.4 GET recommendations (type+target), P.5 POST recommendations/scan (202 enabled / 400 RECOMMENDATION_SCAN_NOT_ENABLED), P.6 GET usage-report (usageReportEnabled gate). Reads require Basic, scan requires Operator; DB-unavailable returns an honest empty 200 (not 500); missing cluster returns 404; missing auth returns 401.

panels.title: PXF test-read Requests (L.15)
panels.description: Request rate + status for GET /data-loading/test-read (cloudberry-ctl data-loading test-read, Scenario 108 L.15) from cloudberry_api_requests_total. test-read reads N rows from a PXF source via a transient external table (honest: available:false when the source/DB is unreachable). No dedicated metric is recorded by the handler — this is the API request signal.

panels.title: DB Query Duration p99 by Operation

options.content:
## Scenario 101 — gpfdist Deployment + gpload-csv CronJob

### gpfdist Deployment / Service / PVC

The operator creates a **gpfdist Deployment** (`<cluster>-gpfdist`), a **Service** (`<cluster>-gpfdist-svc` on port 8080), and a **PVC** (`<cluster>-gpfdist-data-pvc`) serving `/data/incoming/*.csv`.

**Observability:** gpfdist Deployment readiness is observed via `kubectl get deploy <cluster>-gpfdist` (NOT a VictoriaMetrics metric — kube-state-metrics is not deployed in the test environment, so `kube_deployment_status_ready_replicas` is unavailable).

### gpload-csv CronJob (Job 4)

The gpload-csv CronJob runs `gpload -f /etc/gpload/gpload-csv.yml` on the cluster image every 30 minutes. The control file (rendered by the operator) reads `gpfdist://<cluster>-gpfdist-svc:8080/incoming/*.csv` and loads `public.raw_data` (mode: insert, format: csv, header: true, encoding: UTF-8, error-limit: 50 + log-errors, preload: truncate, postAction: ANALYZE).

**Metrics (existing `cloudberry_data_loading_*`):**
- `cloudberry_data_loading_job_status{job="gpload-csv"}` — 0=idle, 1=running, **2=success**, 3=failed
- `cloudberry_data_loading_rows_total{job="gpload-csv", source_type="gpfdist"}` — rows loaded into `raw_data`
- `cloudberry_data_loading_errors_total{job="gpload-csv"}` — failed Job runs
- `cloudberry_data_loading_job_duration_seconds{job="gpload-csv"}` — Job wall-clock time
- `cloudberry_data_loading_job_last_success_timestamp{job="gpload-csv"}` — last success Unix ts

These are the **same panels** already shown in the **Data Loading** row above — the gpload-csv job appears there alongside other data-loading jobs. No duplicate panels are needed.

### Planned Metrics (NOT registered)

`cloudberry_gpfdist_*` (connections, bytes served) remain **PLANNED** — gpfdist does not expose a Prometheus-scrapable metrics endpoint. No panel references these metrics.

### Verification

```bash
# gpfdist Deployment ready?
kubectl get deploy -n cloudberry-test gpfdist-test-gpfdist
# gpload CronJob status?
kubectl get cronjob -n cloudberry-test gpfdist-test-gpload-csv
# Data loaded?
kubectl exec -n cloudberry-test gpfdist-test-coordinator-0 -- \
  bash -lc "psql -c 'SELECT count(*) FROM public.raw_data'"
```

panels.title: PXF Service Up per Segment (M.1)
panels.description: cloudberry_pxf_service_up{cluster,namespace,segment_host} — honest per-segment PXF sidecar health (1=ready,0=not), the per-segment disaggregation of cloudberry_pxf_status. Derived from real segment-primary pxf container readiness (Scenario 109 M.1). Kill a segment's pxf -> that host -> 0.

panels.title: Data Loading Bytes Loaded (M.10)
panels.description: cloudberry_data_loading_bytes_total{cluster,namespace,job,source_type} — bytes loaded per job, harvested from a real DATALOAD_BYTES marker (local gpload wc -c). Honest: ABSENT for jobs where no real byte count is available (external/pxf/FDW) — never synthesized (Scenario 109 M.10).

panels.title: PXF Actuator Requests + Latency (M.2/M.3)
panels.description: Real PXF Spring Boot Actuator request rate + p95 latency from http_server_requests_seconds_count/_bucket, scraped from the pxf sidecar /actuator/prometheus on :5888 (Scenario 109 M.2/M.3). NOTE: server/profile/operation labels are not actuator-derivable; count+latency are the honest signals (request labels downgraded to actuator-native uri/method/status).

panels.title: API Cluster Lifecycle Requests Rate
panels.description: Rate of cluster lifecycle/maintenance actions requested via the API (start, stop, restart, reload, activate-standby, rebalance, vacuum, analyze, reindex, config-update) from cloudberry_api_cluster_lifecycle_requests_total{operation,result}.

panels.title: API Workload Operations Rate
panels.description: Rate of workload-management DDL operations via the API (resource_group, resource_queue, rule) from cloudberry_api_workload_operations_total{kind,operation,result}.

panels.title: PXF Sync Requests Rate
panels.description: Rate of PXF servers sync requests by outcome from cloudberry_pxf_sync_total{cluster,namespace,result}. Separate from pxf_servers_changed (which counts only real ConfigMap Data changes).

panels.title: Lifecycle Requests (1h)
panels.description: Total API cluster lifecycle requests in the last hour

panels.title: Workload Ops (1h)
panels.description: Total API workload DDL operations in the last hour

panels.title: PXF Syncs (1h)
panels.description: Total PXF sync requests in the last hour

panels.title: DB Query Duration by Operation (p95)
panels.description: Database operation duration p95 by operation name from cloudberry_db_query_duration_seconds{operation}. Surfaces the 15 DDL method names and other db.Client operations.

panels.title: gpfdist, PXF Extension, Data-Loader & Exporter Role Setup
gpfdist Reconcile Rate by Operation/Result
panels.description: Rate of gpfdist control-plane resource reconcile outcomes (PVC/Deployment/Service create-update or delete) from cloudberry_gpfdist_reconcile_total{cluster,namespace,operation,result}. operation is one of {pvc, deployment, service, delete}; result is success or error.

panels.title: gpfdist Reconciles (1h)
panels.description: Total gpfdist resource reconcile outcomes in the last hour from cloudberry_gpfdist_reconcile_total

panels.title: gpfdist Errors (1h)
panels.description: Total gpfdist reconcile errors in the last hour from cloudberry_gpfdist_reconcile_total{result='error'}

panels.title: PXF Extension Setup Rate by Result
panels.description: Rate of PXF client-extension setup attempt outcomes from cloudberry_pxf_extension_setup_total{cluster,namespace,result}. result is one of {installed, absent, error}: installed when extensions were created, absent when DB reachable but none installed, error on hard failure.

panels.title: PXF Extension Setups (1h)
panels.description: Total PXF extension setup attempts in the last hour from cloudberry_pxf_extension_setup_total

panels.title: PXF Extension Errors (1h)
panels.description: Total PXF extension setup errors in the last hour from cloudberry_pxf_extension_setup_total{result='error'}

panels.title: Data-Loader Role Setup Rate by Result
panels.description: Rate of data-loader role setup attempt outcomes from cloudberry_dataloader_role_setup_total{cluster,namespace,result}. result is one of {success, error}: success when the dedicated data-loader login role was ensured/granted, error on a hard connectivity/setup failure.

panels.title: Data-Loader Role Setups (1h)
panels.description: Total data-loader role setup attempts in the last hour from cloudberry_dataloader_role_setup_total

panels.title: Data-Loader Role Errors (1h)
panels.description: Total data-loader role setup errors in the last hour from cloudberry_dataloader_role_setup_total{result='error'}

panels.title: Exporter Role Setup Rate by Result
panels.description: Rate of metrics-exporter role setup attempt outcomes from cloudberry_exporter_role_setup_total{cluster,namespace,result}. result is one of {success, error}: success when the metrics-exporter DB login role was created/granted, error on a hard connectivity/setup failure.

panels.title: Exporter Role Setups (1h)
panels.description: Total metrics-exporter role setup attempts in the last hour from cloudberry_exporter_role_setup_total

panels.title: Exporter Role Errors (1h)
panels.description: Total metrics-exporter role setup errors in the last hour from cloudberry_exporter_role_setup_total{result='error'}
